rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VERLIS BROWN SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling (1) Responses To Request For Production Of Documents; (2) Responses To Form Interrogatories; And (3) Appearance At Deposition; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Against Reginald B. Smith; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Daniel A. Conrad In Support
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner's Supplement to Notice of Motion seeks verified responses to requests for production of documents and form interrogories. Verified responses were served on October 8, 2013. The Motion also seeks to compel the deposition of Reginald B. Smith. Reginald B. Smith shall be produced for deposition on 10/24/13 between the hours of 11:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m and, if necessary, continuing on 10/25/13 and thereafter until completed. Petitioner's time to have a motion heard, if necessary, to compel further responses to Responses to Form Interrogatories, Requests for Production of Documents or to Deposition Questions is extended until five court days before trial. Any party who objects to the tentative and wishes to argue the matter must notify the court (415-551-3702) and opposing counsel no later than 5:00 p.m. on 10/15/13.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VERLIS BROWN SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Admissions; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Against Reginald B. Smith; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Daniel A. Conrad In Support
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner's Motion to Establish Admissions and for sanctions is denied. Respondent served verified Amended Response to the Requests for Admisison on 10/8/2013. Petitioner's time to have a further motion heard regarding the Amended Response is extended to five court days before trial. Any party who objects to the tentative and wishes to argue the matter must notify the court (415-551-3702) and opposing counsel by 5:00 p.m. on 10/15/13

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER SECOND AMENDED AND RESTATED, HAROLD E. KORF TRUST, DECEASED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Trustees Demurrer To Amended Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provisions, To Impose Constructive Trust, And To Compel Accounting
rulings.Rulings: As to the cause of action to invalidate the trust amendments based on lack of testamentary capacity, the demurrer is overruled. Petitioner has pled sufficient facts. As to the cause of action to invalidate the trust amendments based on undue influence, the demurrer is sustained. Petitioner has represented he will delete this cause of action if allowed to file a second amended petition. As to the cause of action to compel an accounting, the demurrer is sustained without prejudice. The Petitioner is not a beneficiary to the subject trust and currently lacks standing to compel an accounting. Further, Petitioner has represented that this cause of action was inadvertently included in the petition. As to the cause of action for Intentional Interference with Expectation of Inheritance, the demurrer is sustained. Petitioner has an adequate remedy in probate, therefore he is precluded from claiming this tort. SKH for Judge Dearman.
